title: Bramble Mob
description: A parkour-based hostile mob in the Blaze & Breeze family
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/v877UdYynQo" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

This is the mob-only version of the bramble datapack. If you want the bramble structure, that version can be found at https://modrinth.com/datapack/bramble-henge. If you want to add the bramble to the Lush Cave Ruins structure, that can be found at https://modrinth.com/datapack/lush-cave-bramble.

## The Bramble
This datapack adds a custom earth-themed mob that is desinged to be vanilla-friendly in the vein of the blaze and the breeze. The bramble provides a unique parkour-based combat experience—do you have the platforming skill to take it down?

<details>
<summary>Meet the Bramble:</summary>

![An animated bramble mob spinning](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4a4bd26c702b591e0e5beed55d720df2cb76a956.gif)

</details>

The bramble has the following behaviors:

- The bramble will steadily float upwards as you fight, and will float more when damaged.
- The bramble places temporary leaf blocks that you must climb to reach the bramble.
- The bramble attacks by throwing a poisonous azalea bush into the air. You need to move before it lands!
- The bramble slowly descends when not in combat.

### Using the Bramble

If you want to use the bramble in your own structures/datapacks/whatever feel free to do so, and here is what you will need:

- To summon a bramble directly, use the function `keeb_mob_bramble:summon`.
- There are pre-set template pools `keeb_mob_bramble:bramble/mob` and `keeb_mob_bramble:bramble/spawner` for the mob and a spawner respectively: just use the `minecraft:bottom` jigsaw name.
- There is a trial spawner configuration set for the bramble `keeb_mob_bramble:bramble`.

Additionally, if you want even more control, there are a couple of function tags you can use:
- `#keeb_mob_bramble:bramble/spawn` is called once on a Bramble when it spawns
- `#keeb_mob_bramble:bramble/despawn` is called once at a Bramble's last position after it despawns
- `#keeb_mob_bramble:bramble/die` is called once at a Bramble's last position after it dies
